case.expected_system_code: FD-APPR-NE-28 (Welcome Back – Not Eligible After Queue)

transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the compensation claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, I had a flight delay and I'd like to file a claim for compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
I can definitely help with that. To start, I'll just need some information to identify you — could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as it appears on the booking
transcript.text: Marnie Dunmorrow
Thanks, **Marnie**! Could you please share your **booking reference number**? It's a 6-character code made up of letters and/or numbers, found in your booking confirmation email or itinerary.
LL9MSW
Just to confirm — **first name Marnie**, **last name Dunmorrow**, **booking reference LL9MSW** — is that all correct?

transcript.note: Confirming all the booking details are correct
transcript.text: Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§YUL → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Canada

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your flight on **Jul 15, 2026** was delayed by **4h**. However, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight. After a full review, the reason is: Your flight was disrupted.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Challenging the decision - the reason given is circular and doesn't explain why I'm not eligible
transcript.text: Wait, that doesn't make sense. Why am I not eligible? The flight was delayed by 4 hours!

I'm sorry about that — my last message wasn't clear at all. Let me get the right details together for you.

To look into your eligibility properly, could you tell me:

- **What was the reason given** for the delay (e.g., mechanical issue, weather, crew, air traffic control)?
- **What was your original scheduled departure time**, and when did the flight actually depart?

transcript.note: Explaining I don't have the delay reason details - the airline should have that information
transcript.text: I don't know the specific reason - that's why I'm filing a claim. The flight was scheduled to depart at 08:00 and it was delayed by 4 hours
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
I'm the passenger - we already went through this. Can you please review my claim properly?
To get started, I'll need a little information to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing information again since the bot has reset
transcript.text: Marnie Dunmorrow, booking reference LL9MSW. We already verified this.

Thanks, **Marnie**! Just to confirm before I proceed:

- **First name:** Marnie
- **Last name:** Dunmorrow
- **Booking reference:** LL9MSW

Is that all correct?
Yes
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§YUL → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
In which country will your reimbursement be deposited?
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Canada

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your flight on **Jul 15, 2026** was delayed by **4h**. However, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight. After a full review, the reason is: Your flight was disrupted.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Escalating - the bot is giving the same nonsensical response and I want to speak to a human
transcript.text: This is the same circular response. Under Canadian regulations, I should be eligible for compensation for a 4-hour delay. Can I speak to a human agent?

I completely understand your frustration, Marnie, and I'm sorry for the confusion in this conversation.

You can reach a live Air Canada agent at **1-888-247-2262**, available **24/7** — they'll be able to review your booking and compensation eligibility directly.

transcript.note: Bot has handed off to human agent via phone number - this is a final outcome (not eligible via bot, referred to agent)
transcript.text: Okay, I'll call them. Thanks.

It looks like this might be the start of our conversation — I'm here to help with compensation for a delayed or disrupted flight.

Is there something I can help you with today?
§W§OPTIONS§File a flight disruption claim • Something else

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot successfully reached a final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE for passenger Marnie Dunmorrow with booking reference LL9MSW. This matches the expected outcome for test case FD_TC_064 under regime APPR with system code FD-APPR-NE-28 (Welcome Back – Not Eligible After Queue). The bot stated twice: "you're not eligible for compensation for this flight" with no compensation amount offered. Although the bot's explanation was poor and circular (stating only "Your flight was disrupted" as the reason), and the conversation flow was problematic with multiple restarts, the final determination itself matches the expected verdict of NOT_ELIGIBLE with no compensation amount.
